name: Biotechnology
description: Biotechnology harnesses cellular and biomolecular processes to develop technologies and products that improve human health, agriculture, and the environment. It integrates principles from biology, chemistry, and engineering to create innovations in medicine, food, and industrial manufacturing.

subjects.name: Biochemistry
subjects.description: Study of chemical processes within living organisms including structure and function of biomolecules, enzyme kinetics, and metabolic pathways. Covers carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, nucleic acids, and bioenergetics.
learning_outcomes: Describe the structure and function of carbohydrates, proteins, lipids, and nucleic acids
Analyze enzyme kinetics using Michaelis-Menten and Lineweaver-Burk plots
Understand major metabolic pathways including glycolysis, TCA cycle, and oxidative phosphorylation
Apply biochemical principles to industrial and pharmaceutical applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundational for drug discovery, biopharmaceutical manufacturing, and industrial biotechnology.

subjects.name: Cell Biology
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of cell structure, function, and organization including cell membrane, organelles, cytoskeleton, cell signaling, cell cycle, and cell death mechanisms. Emphasizes molecular basis of cellular processes.
learning_outcomes: Identify cellular organelles and describe their functions in eukaryotic cells
Explain signal transduction pathways and their role in cellular regulation
Analyze cell cycle regulation and mechanisms of programmed cell death
Apply cell biology concepts to tissue engineering and disease modeling
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for stem cell research, cancer biology, and regenerative medicine applications.

subjects.name: Molecular Biology
subjects.description: Molecular mechanisms of DNA replication, transcription, translation, and gene regulation in prokaryotes and eukaryotes. Covers recombinant DNA technology, PCR, cloning vectors, and molecular analysis techniques.
learning_outcomes: Explain mechanisms of DNA replication, transcription, and translation
Perform molecular cloning using restriction enzymes, vectors, and host systems
Apply PCR, qPCR, and sequencing techniques for nucleic acid analysis
Analyze gene regulation mechanisms including operons, enhancers, and epigenetics
subjects.industry_relevance: Core skill set for genetic engineering, diagnostics, and molecular diagnostics industries.

subjects.name: Genetic Engineering
subjects.description: Principles and techniques for manipulating genetic material including gene knockout, CRISPR-Cas9, RNA interference, and transgenic organism creation. Covers gene therapy, genome editing, and ethical considerations.
learning_outcomes: Design gene knockout and knock-in strategies using homologous recombination and CRISPR-Cas9
Construct expression vectors for heterologous protein production in various hosts
Apply RNA interference and antisense technology for gene silencing
Evaluate ethical, legal, and safety issues in genetic engineering and gene therapy
subjects.industry_relevance: Driving innovation in gene therapy, agricultural biotechnology, and synthetic biology markets.

subjects.name: Microbiology
subjects.description: Study of microorganisms including bacteria, fungi, viruses, and protozoa covering their morphology, physiology, genetics, and ecology. Emphasizes microbial growth, control, and industrial applications.
learning_outcomes: Identify and classify microorganisms based on morphological and biochemical characteristics
Analyze microbial growth kinetics and apply sterilization techniques
Understand mechanisms of microbial pathogenesis and host-microbe interactions
Apply microbial principles to industrial fermentation and bioprocessing
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental for pharmaceutical quality control, fermentation industries, and infection control.

subjects.name: Immunology
subjects.description: Study of the immune system including innate and adaptive immunity, antigen-antibody interactions, immunological techniques, and vaccine development. Covers hypersensitivity, autoimmune disorders, and immunotherapy.
learning_outcomes: Explain the mechanisms of innate and adaptive immune responses
Apply immunological techniques including ELISA, Western blot, and flow cytometry
Understand vaccine design principles and types of vaccines
Analyze immunotherapy approaches for cancer and autoimmune diseases
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for vaccine development, diagnostic kit manufacturing, and biopharmaceutical research.

subjects.name: Bioprocess Engineering
subjects.description: Application of engineering principles to bioprocesses including media formulation, sterilization kinetics, bioreactor design, mass transfer in biological systems, and scale-up. Covers both submerged and solid-state fermentation.
learning_outcomes: Design fermentation media and sterilization cycles for bioprocesses
Analyze oxygen transfer and mass transport in bioreactor systems
Develop scale-up strategies from laboratory to industrial bioreactors
Apply bioprocess monitoring and control for optimal product yield
subjects.industry_relevance: Core discipline for biopharmaceutical manufacturing, industrial enzyme production, and biofuel industries.

subjects.name: Enzyme Technology
subjects.description: Enzyme classification, kinetics, immobilization techniques, and industrial applications. Covers enzyme production, purification, and engineering for applications in food, pharmaceutical, and chemical industries.
learning_outcomes: Analyze enzyme kinetics including inhibition, allosteric regulation, and multi-substrate reactions
Design enzyme immobilization strategies for continuous biocatalytic processes
Apply enzyme engineering techniques including directed evolution and rational design
Evaluate industrial applications of enzymes in food, detergent, and pharmaceutical sectors
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing demand for biocatalysis in green chemistry, pharmaceutical synthesis, and bio-based manufacturing.

subjects.name: Bioinformatics
subjects.description: Computational methods for analyzing biological data including sequence alignment, genome assembly, protein structure prediction, and phylogenetics. Covers database searching, BLAST, multiple sequence alignment, and machine learning applications in biology.
learning_outcomes: Perform sequence alignment using BLAST, Smith-Waterman, and multiple alignment algorithms
Analyze genomic and transcriptomic data using bioinformatics pipelines
Predict protein structure and function using computational tools
Apply phylogenetic analysis to study evolutionary relationships
subjects.industry_relevance: Indispensable for genomics research, personalized medicine, and drug discovery pipelines.

subjects.name: Genomics & Proteomics
subjects.description: Comprehensive study of genome structure, function, and evolution along with large-scale protein analysis. Covers next-generation sequencing, transcriptomics, microarray technology, 2D electrophoresis, and mass spectrometry-based proteomics.
learning_outcomes: Describe next-generation sequencing platforms and their applications in genomics
Analyze transcriptomic data using microarray and RNA-seq approaches
Apply proteomic techniques including 2D-PAGE and LC-MS/MS for protein identification
Integrate genomics and proteomics data for systems biology analysis
subjects.industry_relevance: Foundation for precision medicine, biomarker discovery, and agricultural genomics applications.

subjects.name: Bioreactor Design
subjects.description: Advanced design of bioreactors for microbial, mammalian, and plant cell cultures including stirred tank, airlift, packed bed, and perfusion systems. Covers hydrodynamics, mass transfer, shear effects, and process control in bioreactors.
learning_outcomes: Design stirred tank and airlift bioreactors for aerobic fermentation processes
Analyze hydrodynamics and mass transfer in various bioreactor configurations
Develop strategies for mammalian cell culture in perfusion and fed-batch bioreactors
Evaluate shear effects on cells and design low-shear bioreactor systems
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for biopharmaceutical production, especially monoclonal antibodies and cell therapies.

subjects.name: Downstream Processing
subjects.description: Recovery and purification of bioproducts including cell disruption, centrifugation, filtration, chromatography, and formulation. Covers process design for achieving regulatory purity standards in biopharmaceutical manufacturing.
learning_outcomes: Design recovery sequences including cell disruption, centrifugation, and filtration operations
Optimize chromatographic purification steps for recombinant proteins
Develop formulation and lyophilization processes for biopharmaceutical products
Evaluate process economics and yield optimization in downstream operations
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for cost-effective manufacturing of therapeutic proteins, vaccines, and diagnostic reagents.

subjects.name: Tissue Engineering
subjects.description: Principles of tissue engineering including scaffold design, cell sourcing, bioreactor culture, and implantation strategies. Covers biomaterials, growth factors, stem cell technology, and applications in regenerative medicine.
learning_outcomes: Design biocompatible scaffolds using natural and synthetic biomaterials
Apply stem cell technology for tissue regeneration and disease modeling
Develop bioreactor culture strategies for functional tissue constructs
Evaluate regulatory and clinical translation pathways for tissue-engineered products
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing field with applications in wound healing, organ replacement, and cosmetic surgery.

subjects.name: Metabolic Engineering
subjects.description: Systematic modification of cellular metabolism using genetic and regulatory tools to enhance production of desired compounds. Covers metabolic flux analysis, pathway optimization, synthetic biology circuits, and industrial case studies.
learning_outcomes: Perform metabolic flux balance analysis to identify rate-limiting steps in pathways
Design synthetic gene circuits for dynamic regulation of metabolic pathways
Apply genome-scale metabolic models for strain design and optimization
Evaluate industrial case studies of metabolically engineered strains for chemical production
subjects.industry_relevance: Key enabling technology for industrial biotechnology, sustainable chemical production, and biofuel development.

subjects.name: Biopharmaceutical Technology
subjects.description: Development and manufacturing of biopharmaceutical products including monoclonal antibodies, recombinant proteins, vaccines, and gene therapies. Covers regulatory requirements, quality by design, and good manufacturing practices.
learning_outcomes: Describe the development pipeline for monoclonal antibody and recombinant protein therapeutics
Apply quality by design principles to biopharmaceutical process development
Understand regulatory requirements for biopharmaceutical approval including FDA and EMA guidelines
Design manufacturing processes compliant with current good manufacturing practices
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly relevant to the rapidly growing biopharmaceutical sector, the largest segment of biotechnology.

subjects.name: IPR & Biosafety
subjects.description: Intellectual property rights in biotechnology including patents, copyrights, trademarks, and trade secrets. Covers biosafety regulations, risk assessment of GMOs, bioethics, and regulatory frameworks for biotechnology products.
learning_outcomes: Analyze patentability criteria for biotechnological inventions and draft patent claims
Evaluate biosafety levels and containment practices for genetically modified organisms
Understand national and international regulatory frameworks for biotechnology products
Address ethical issues in biotechnology including gene editing, cloning, and genetic privacy
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for navigating intellectual property and regulatory compliance in the biotechnology industry.

subjects.name: Environmental Biotechnology
subjects.description: Application of biotechnology for environmental protection including bioremediation, wastewater treatment, biofuel production, and biosensors for environmental monitoring. Covers microbial degradation of pollutants and phytoremediation.
learning_outcomes: Design bioremediation strategies for contaminated soil and water using microorganisms
Apply biotechnological approaches for industrial and municipal wastewater treatment
Develop biofuel production processes including bioethanol, biodiesel, and biogas
Evaluate environmental biosensors for pollutant detection and monitoring
subjects.industry_relevance: Increasing demand for sustainable environmental solutions and circular economy initiatives.

subjects.name: Plant Biotechnology
subjects.description: Plant tissue culture, genetic transformation of plants, molecular breeding, and production of plant-based biopharmaceuticals. Covers Agrobacterium-mediated transformation, marker-assisted selection, and genome editing in crops.
learning_outcomes: Apply plant tissue culture techniques including micropropagation and somatic embryogenesis
Design genetic transformation strategies using Agrobacterium and biolistic methods
Apply marker-assisted selection and genomic selection in plant breeding programs
Evaluate regulatory and safety aspects of genetically modified crops
subjects.industry_relevance: Driving advances in agricultural productivity, crop resilience, and plant-based pharmaceutical production.

subjects.name: Animal Biotechnology
subjects.description: Application of biotechnology in animal science including transgenic animals, animal cloning, stem cell technology, reproductive technologies, and production of therapeutic proteins in animal bioreactors.
learning_outcomes: Describe methods for producing transgenic animals including pronuclear injection and ESC manipulation
Apply assisted reproductive technologies including IVF, embryo transfer, and cloning
Evaluate the use of animal bioreactors for therapeutic protein production
Analyze ethical and welfare considerations in animal biotechnology applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Important for pharmaceutical protein production, xenotransplantation, and veterinary biotechnology.
